public_notes: BRender is the first production-readiness pilot. A public source checkout matched the recorded v1.3.2 snapshot commit, exposed period make/project metadata, and now has a materializer plus successful out-of-tree CMake configure, FLOAT core-library build, vector math runtime smoke, BrBegin/BrEnd framework startup smoke, a wireframe render smoke, and a v1db scene-graph render smoke through CTest under a Visual Studio Win32 target. The render smoke projects a hand-built cube through the engine matrix pipeline; the scene smoke goes further and renders a model out of BRender's own scene database, building a world/camera/model actor tree, preparing a br_model with BrModelUpdate, and projecting it with the engine's BrActorToScreenMatrix4 before drawing the faces with BrPixelmapLine. Both draw entirely through the pure-C memory pixelmap dispatch with no assembly software driver, verify pixels by read-back, and dump a 320x240 PPM. The produced brender_core_float.lib, smoke executables, and PPM output remain outside this metadata repo. x64 portability, shaded and textured scene rendering (which needs the softrend renderer device), datafile model loading, warning cleanup, packaging, drivers, and FIXED variants are not claimed yet.
